mainEntity.name: What is AQA?

acceptedAnswer.text: AQA (AI Question Answer) is an open specification that enriches Schema.org FAQPage structured data with metadata that AI systems need to assess answer quality, freshness, and provenance. It uses a custom JSON-LD context to add extension properties like per-question dates, citations, changelogs, and AI usage policies to existing Schema.org types. AQA is MIT-licensed, free forever, and has no vendor lock-in.

mainEntity.ragSummary: AQA is an open, MIT-licensed specification extending Schema.org FAQPage with AI-optimized metadata: per-question dates, citations, changelogs, usage policies. No vendor lock-in.

changelog.changeDescription: Initial FAQ entry created for aqa-spec.org launch

mainEntity.name: Is AQA compatible with Schema.org?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. AQA uses only existing Schema.org types (Article, FAQPage, Question, Answer) and adds extension properties through a JSON-LD context. Validators that do not understand the AQA namespace silently ignore the extra properties. Existing Schema.org markup continues to work unchanged. AQA is a strict superset — every valid AQA block is also a valid Schema.org document.

mainEntity.ragSummary: AQA is fully Schema.org compatible. It extends existing types via JSON-LD context. Unknown properties are silently ignored. Every AQA block is a valid Schema.org document.

changelog.changeDescription: Initial FAQ entry created for aqa-spec.org launch

mainEntity.name: What are the AQA conformance levels?

acceptedAnswer.text: AQA defines three conformance levels. Basic requires per-question dates, at least one citation per answer, and document-level author and language. Standard adds update frequency, changelog, question versioning, sector classification, and structured citations. Full requires monitoring sources, per-question authorship with credentials, and complete change provenance. All levels are cumulative — Standard includes everything in Basic, and Full includes everything in Standard.

mainEntity.ragSummary: Three cumulative levels: Basic (dates + citations), Standard (changelog + versioning + sector), Full (monitoring sources + per-question authors + full provenance).

changelog.changeDescription: Initial FAQ entry created for aqa-spec.org launch

mainEntity.name: What is AQA Shield?

acceptedAnswer.text: AQA Shield is an orthogonal protection mechanism available at any conformance level. A document qualifies for AQA Shield when every question includes both an aiUsagePolicy (declaring permitted AI uses such as RAG citation, training, summarization, quoting, and commercial use) and a contentSignature (SHA-256 hash proving answer integrity). Shield combines legal declaration with cryptographic verification.

mainEntity.ragSummary: AQA Shield = aiUsagePolicy + contentSignature on all questions. Combines legal AI usage declaration with SHA-256 integrity proof. Works at any conformance level.

changelog.changeDescription: Initial FAQ entry created for aqa-spec.org launch

mainEntity.name: How do I implement AQA on my website?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a JSON-LD script block to your FAQ page with a dual context referencing both Schema.org and the AQA context at https://aqa-spec.org/ns/context.jsonld. Wrap your FAQ in an Article containing a FAQPage, add per-question dateCreated, dateModified, and citation properties, then validate with the Python validator. The migration guide on GitHub provides 8 detailed steps from bare HTML FAQ to full AQA implementation.

mainEntity.ragSummary: Add JSON-LD with dual context (Schema.org + AQA). Wrap FAQ in Article > FAQPage. Add per-question dates and citations. Validate with Python validator. See migration guide.

changelog.changeDescription: Initial FAQ entry created for aqa-spec.org launch
